risk_model.chain: Hazard → Sequence (P1) → Hazardous Situation → Harm (S, P2)
risk_model.p1_description: Probability sequence leads to hazardous situation
risk_model.p2_description: Probability hazardous situation results in specific harm
risk_model.rpn_calculation: RPN = S × P (where P derived from P1 × P2)

hazard_assessments.description: Wrong patient data displayed in search or triage results

sequences_of_events.name: Race condition in patient search
steps: 1. Practitioner searches for patient by name
2. Multiple patients with similar names returned
4. API response for patient B arrives late and overwrites display
5. Practitioner views patient B's data as patient A
sequences_of_events.P1_rationale: Occasional: Race conditions possible with slow network

sequences_of_events.name: Practitioner selects wrong patient
sequences_of_events.P1_rationale: Probable: Human error common with similar names

hazardous_situations.description: Practitioner conducts clinical decision-making using wrong patient's data

harms.description: Patient receives contraindicated treatment based on wrong history/allergies
harms.S_rationale: Catastrophic: Contraindicated treatment could cause death
harms.P2_rationale: Remote: Contraindication match between wrong patient's data and correct patient's condition unlikely

harms.description: Urgent condition missed - wrong patient's symptoms appear non-urgent
harms.S_rationale: Critical: Delayed urgent care could cause permanent impairment
harms.P2_rationale: Occasional: Symptom mismatch may hide urgency in ~10% of cases

harms.description: Inappropriate care routing delays needed treatment
harms.S_rationale: Serious: Delay may require additional intervention but recoverable
harms.P2_rationale: Probable: Wrong data likely leads to suboptimal routing

risk_estimation.note: Using S=5 from worst harm, P1=3 from worst sequence, P2=2 from that harm

sequences_of_events.name: Urgency indicator not visible in busy workflow
steps: 1. Patient completes triage with urgent symptoms
3. Result displays urgency (small, low contrast, poorly positioned)
5. Practitioner does not notice urgency indicator
sequences_of_events.P1_rationale: Probable: Usability unverified, high workload normal

sequences_of_events.name: Color-coded indicator not distinguishable
steps: 3. Practitioner has color vision deficiency (~8% males)

hazardous_situations.description: Practitioner makes care routing decision unaware of patient's urgency

harms.description: Urgent condition deteriorates due to delayed treatment
harms.S_rationale: Critical: Delayed urgent care could cause permanent impairment
harms.P2_rationale: Occasional: Missed urgency leads to inappropriate routing ~10-50%

harms.description: Patient experiences unnecessary suffering from delayed care
harms.P2_rationale: Probable: Delayed care almost always causes some suffering

sequences_of_events.name: Malicious SQL injection attack
steps: 1. Attacker identifies API endpoint accepting labels
5. If escapeString fails, payload reaches SQL
sequences_of_events.P1_rationale: Occasional: Requires attacker access, escapeString protection UNVERIFIED

sequences_of_events.name: Internal malformed label
steps: 1. Internal system generates label with SQL metacharacters
sequences_of_events.P1_rationale: Remote: Internal systems generate safe labels typically

harms.description: Mass patient data breach - PHI exposed
harms.S_rationale: Catastrophic: Privacy breach affecting all patients
harms.P2_rationale: Probable: If injection succeeds, exfiltration straightforward

harms.S_rationale: Catastrophic: Loss of patient data affects all patients
harms.P2_rationale: Occasional: Attacker may corrupt/delete, but exfiltration usually primary goal

harms.S_rationale: Critical: Lateral movement to other healthcare systems
harms.P2_rationale: Occasional: DB access may enable privilege escalation

harms.description: Wrong patient data returned causing clinical error
harms.S_rationale: Critical: Wrong data could lead to wrong decision
harms.P2_rationale: Remote: Malformed query more likely to fail than return plausible wrong data

sequences_of_events.name: Interviewer fails to collect attribute
steps: 4. Incomplete data passed to rule engine

hazardous_situations.description: Rule engine evaluates triage without critical patient attribute

harms.description: Age-dependent urgent condition triaged as non-urgent
harms.S_rationale: Critical: Missing age could miss pediatric/geriatric thresholds
harms.P2_rationale: Remote: Most rules fail gracefully or produce obvious errors

harms.S_rationale: Serious: Gender affects limited subset of assessments

sequences_of_events.name: Session context confusion
steps: 4. Patient B triggers action with Patient A's context
sequences_of_events.P1_rationale: Occasional: Shared devices common in clinical settings

hazardous_situations.description: Care action executed for wrong patient based on another's triage

harms.description: Wrong patient receives care appointment based on another's triage
harms.S_rationale: Critical: Wrong care type for wrong patient
harms.P2_rationale: Occasional: Downstream systems may not detect mismatch

harms.description: Correct patient does not receive needed care
harms.P2_rationale: Occasional: Original patient may notice and re-triage

residual_risk.P2_residual_rationale: Remote: Booking systems have additional verification

hazard_assessments.description: Patient enters incorrect symptom data due to confusing UI

sequences_of_events.name: Confusing question wording
sequences_of_events.P1_rationale: Probable: Medical terminology common, B1-B2 language users

sequences_of_events.name: Accidental wrong selection
sequences_of_events.P1_rationale: Occasional: Touch errors common on mobile

hazardous_situations.description: Triage logic evaluates incorrect patient-reported symptoms

harms.description: Patient directed to lower urgency care than needed
harms.S_rationale: Serious: May delay needed treatment but typically recoverable
harms.P2_rationale: Occasional: Wrong symptom affects triage in ~10-50% of cases

harms.description: Patient directed to higher urgency than needed (over-triage)
harms.P2_rationale: Occasional: Over-triage also possible from incorrect input
